mission.title: n8n: installing community nodes get "spawn npm ENOENT" Issue in n8n on Windows
mission.description: installing community nodes get "spawn npm ENOENT" Issue in n8n on Windows. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

When installing community nodes in n8n under the Windows environment, the "spawn npm ENOENT" error often occurs. The core reason is that n8n does not adapt to Windows script suffix resolution when calling npm.

steps.title: Apply the fix for installing community nodes get "spawn npm ENOENT" Issue in…
steps.description:
I encountered the same issue on the current version (**n8n.9.4**) installed on Windows via `npx n8n`.

The solution was to add `shell: true` to the `asyncExecFile` options in the following
```yaml
error Using Original file
Failed to execute npm command
Error loading package "n8n-nodes-kommo" :Failed to execute npm command
Cause: spawn npm ENOENT
ResponseError: Error loading package "n8n-nodes-kommo" :Failed to execute npm command
Cause: spawn npm ENOENT
    at CommunityPackagesController.installPackage (C:\Users\AC\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\n8n\src\modules\community-packages\community-packages.controller.ts:135:10)
    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)
    at handler (C:\Users\AC\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\n8n\src\controller.registry.ts:
```

steps.title: Upgrade n8n to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade n8n:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade n8n n8n/n8n --namespace n8n
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n
helm list -n n8n
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "installing community nodes get "spawn npm ENOENT" Issue in…" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
